question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/TransactionRepository.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: findByConsensusTimestampBetween()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a large volume of on-chain transactions to inflate the transaction table, then trigger a caller of findByConsensusTimestampBetween() with a maximally wide timestamp range (startInclusive=0, endInclusive=Long.MAX_VALUE) and an unbounded Pageable, causing the JVM heap to exhaust loading the entire List<Transaction> result set into memory and crashing the importer process?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/TransactionRepository.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: findByConsensusTimestampBetween()] Can an unprivileged external user, by flooding the network with transactions over a sustained period, cause the transaction table to grow to a size where any call to findByConsensusTimestampBetween() with a wide range forces a full sequential scan of the partitioned transaction table, consuming all available database CPU and blocking other importer operations?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/TransactionRepository.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: findByConsensusTimestampBetween()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a maximum page size enforcement on the Pageable parameter passed to findByConsensusTimestampBetween() to trigger an arbitrarily large result set allocation in the importer JVM, leading to OutOfMemoryError and service crash?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/TransactionRepository.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: prune(long consensusTimestamp)] Can an unprivileged external user, by submitting a high volume of transactions to the network, cause the prune() operation to attempt a single unbounded DELETE FROM transaction WHERE consensusTimestamp <= ?1 across billions of rows, holding an exclusive table/partition lock for an extended period and starving all concurrent read and write operations on the transaction table?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/TransactionRepository.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: prune(long consensusTimestamp)] Can an unprivileged external user flood the network with transactions so that when prune() executes its @Modifying DELETE query, the transaction log (WAL) grows unboundedly, exhausting disk space on the database host and causing a full system DoS for the importer and all dependent services?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/TransactionRepository.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: prune(long consensusTimestamp)] Can an unprivileged external user cause repeated triggering of prune() with a consensusTimestamp value of Long.MAX_VALUE (if the caller does not validate the upper bound), resulting in deletion of all transaction records and causing a permanent data-availability DoS for downstream consumers of the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/TransactionRepository.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: prune(long consensusTimestamp)] Can an unprivileged external user, by generating a burst of transactions that fills multiple partition ranges, cause the prune() DELETE to cross partition boundaries simultaneously, triggering partition-level lock escalation in PostgreSQL and blocking all concurrent importer writes for the duration of the bulk delete?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/TransactionRepository.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: findByConsensusTimestampBetween()] Can an unprivileged external user, by observing that findByConsensusTimestampBetween() returns a List<Transaction> (not a Stream or Page), cause the caller to load all matching rows into heap memory simultaneously, and by pre-populating the table with millions of transactions in a target timestamp range, trigger a JVM OutOfMemoryError that kills the importer?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/TransactionRepository.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: findByConsensusTimestampBetween()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the transaction table is partitioned by range on consensus_timestamp, and by crafting a timestamp range that spans many partitions, force findByConsensusTimestampBetween() to perform a cross-partition merge scan that consumes disproportionate database memory and CPU, causing a non-network DoS?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/TransactionRepository.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: prune(long consensusTimestamp)] Can an unprivileged external user cause the prune() @Transactional DELETE to hold an open database transaction for an extended period while deleting millions of rows, exhausting the database's max_connections or connection pool, thereby denying service to all other importer and REST API database operations?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/TransactionRepository.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: findByConsensusTimestampBetween()] Can an unprivileged external user, by submitting transactions with carefully chosen consensus timestamps that cluster densely in a single partition, cause findByConsensusTimestampBetween() to return a result set so large that JDBC result set buffering is bypassed and the entire result is materialized in JVM heap, causing GC pressure and eventual OOM?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/TransactionRepository.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: findByConsensusTimestampBetween()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the lack of a hard cap on the Pageable page size in findByConsensusTimestampBetween() to request Integer.MAX_VALUE rows per page, causing the database to attempt to sort and return an unbounded result set, exhausting database work_mem and crashing the PostgreSQL backend process?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/TransactionRepository.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: prune(long consensusTimestamp)] Can an unprivileged external user, by generating transactions that populate many child partitions of the partitioned transaction table, cause the prune() DELETE to trigger partition pruning logic that iterates over all child partitions, causing O(n_partitions) planning overhead that makes each prune() call take exponentially longer as partitions accumulate?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/TransactionRepository.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: prune(long consensusTimestamp)] Can an unprivileged external user, by submitting transactions with transaction_bytes and transaction_record_bytes fields at maximum size, cause the prune() DELETE to generate a WAL entry so large per row that WAL archiving falls behind, filling the pg_wal directory and causing PostgreSQL to halt all write operations?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/TransactionRepository.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: findByConsensusTimestampBetween()] Can an unprivileged external user, by submitting transactions with maximum-length memo fields (100 bytes each) at a rate that fills the transaction table, cause findByConsensusTimestampBetween() to return a result set whose total byte size exceeds the JDBC fetch buffer, causing repeated round-trips to the database that hold a database cursor open indefinitely and exhaust cursor resources?
